title: Dispatcher: split task-dispatcher.mjs into focused modules
description:
task-dispatcher.mjs is 2,349 lines and handles too many concerns: task loading, state merging, dead session recovery, stale claim release, dependency resolution, dispatch, hook polling, push/PR creation, stage validation, notification, and logging. This makes individual functions hard to test and the file difficult to navigate.

Proposed split into `operations/bin/dispatcher/` directory:
- `loader.mjs` — loadTasks(), applyRuntimeTaskState(), shouldPreferCanonicalControlState(), normalizeTerminalTasks()
- `claims.mjs` — inspectActiveHookClaims(), isSessionDead(), recoverDeadSession(), isClaimStale()
- `github.mjs` — runGh(), maybePushAndCreatePR(), collectTaskGitEvidence(), ensureSpecialistRepoView()
- `validation.mjs` — validateStageCompletion(), pickExecutor(), statusForDispatch()
- `notify.mjs` — notifyAndRecord(), appendAlert(), appendLog()
- `dispatch.mjs` — dispatchTask(), postHookWithCurl(), hookPoll()
- `index.mjs` — main(), argument parsing, orchestration

Keep the existing `task-dispatcher.mjs` as a thin entry point that imports and calls `index.mjs` for backwards compatibility with the systemd service.

Do not change any function signatures or behavior — this is a pure refactor.
handoff_notes:
[agent:engineer][task:ops-0045] implemented successfully.

Files changed:
- operations/bin/dispatcher/index.mjs: clean orchestration layer, all imports at top, duplicate function declarations removed, main() uses dispatchTask() from dispatch.mjs
- All 7 modules pass node --check: claims.mjs, dispatch.mjs, github.mjs, index.mjs, loader.mjs, notify.mjs, validation.mjs
- operations/bin/task-dispatcher.mjs: thin entry point unchanged

Commit: 5e1f7db on branch agent/engineer/ops-0045-dispatcher-modular-split

artifacts.description: Clean orchestration layer: all imports at top, removed duplicate function declarations already in dispatch.mjs/claims.mjs, main() delegates dispatch loop to dispatchTask()
